title: lilium × batemanniae
intro: Lilium × batemanniae, also known as the Madam Meckel's trumpet or the Meckel's lily, is a hybrid of the Lilium candidum and Lilium auratum, characterized by its showy, trumpet-shaped flowers in shades of white and pink. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Lilium × batemanniae include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying the flowers and leaves, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be consumed for their medicinal properties.
